payload.input: "Still healing," Vera observes, looking up at you. "Shame. I wanted to fuck the hole that pushed out your kid."  She stands and retrieves something from her jacket pocket—a metal clamp, the kind used for irrigation tubing or livestock tagging. She attaches it to Maya’s free nipple, the one not currently in the calf’s mouth, screwing it tight until the flesh bulges purple and milk spurts in rhythmic jets onto the straw. "Let’s see how much you’ve got in there," Vera says, and begins to strike Maya’s breasts with the flat of her hand, alternating sides, the impacts making the milk spray in chaotic patterns. The baby detaches with a cry, and Vera catches the infant with surprising gentleness, holding it out to Maya. "Put her back on. Don’t let her fall."
